summarization.a: The chart shows the growth per cycle and lithium concentration in at% as a function of lithium tert-butoxide pulse time at a deposition temperature of 225°C. Both curves show saturation behavior.

data_extraction.a:
| Lithium tert-butoxide pulse time (s)' | Growth per cycle (Å/cycle) | Lithium concentration (at.%) |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 0.28 | 0.01 |
| 2 | 0.37 | 0.08 |
| 4 | 0.43 | 0.15 |
| 8 | 0.47 | 0.20 |
| 12 | 0.48 | 0.21 |

a.question: Is the process self-limiting, based on the plot?
When does GPC reach a plateau?
When does lithium concentration reach a plateau?
At a pulse time of 4 seconds, has the process reached saturation? What supports this conclusion?
a.answer: No. At 4 seconds, both the growth rate (~0.43 Å/cycle) and lithium concentration (~15 at.%) are still rising steeply and are clearly lower than the values observed at 8 and 12 seconds. This indicates that 4 seconds is insufficient.
